title: Egeria — pilgrimage to the Holy Land

intro.title: The Lady's Itinerarium
intro.body: Egeria, a Hispano-Roman nun, travelled from Gallaecia to the Holy Land and Sinai over three years. The surviving Itinerarium Egeriae records her route in letters home: 'Don't think, sisters, that the place is small.' Her descriptions of the Sinai monasteries and Jerusalem liturgy are the earliest extant.

stops.name: Constantinople
stops.note: Egeria's letter-receiving sisters were in Gallaecia; she writes from Constantinople on the return leg, c. 384.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. New capital with Hagia Eirene under scaffolding, walls along the Marmara, woman in plain habit at a scriptorium window.

stops.name: Jerusalem
stops.note: Egeria's base for the central year of her journey. She records Holy Week liturgy at the Anastasis and the Mount of Olives.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Constantinian Anastasis rotunda, processional cross, pilgrims with candles in narrow lanes.

stops.name: Mount Sinai
stops.note: Itinerarium I-V: Egeria climbs the mountain at dawn, hears the Exodus story read at the summit, descends to the burning-bush garden.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Granite pinnacle in dawn light, walled monastery in the wadi below, single small figure on a ridge.

stops.name: Edessa
stops.note: Itinerarium XVII-XIX: Egeria visits the tomb of the apostle Thomas and receives the legendary Abgar-Christ correspondence.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. North-Mesopotamian city with citadel pools, basilica with martyrium shrine.

stops.name: Chalcedon
stops.note: Itinerarium XXIII: Egeria's last extant stop — she crosses from the Asian side back to Constantinople at the Euphemia martyrium.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Bosphorus ferry landing, octagonal martyrium of Saint Euphemia, pilgrims kissing relics.
